meta.summary: WebLogo 3.9.0 runtime for generating sequence logos from biological sequence alignments and motif matrices.
meta.description: WebLogo 3.9.0 runtime for generating sequence logos from biological sequence alignments and motif matrices.

test: weblogo --version 2>&1 | grep -Fx 'WebLogo 3.9.0' >/dev/null && transformseq --version 2>&1 | grep -Fx '1.0.0' >/dev/null && weblogo --help 2>&1 | grep -F 'Format of output: eps' >/dev/null && weblogo --help 2>&1 | grep -F 'WebLogo Server' >/dev/null && transformseq --help 2>&1 | grep -F 'Multiple sequence alignment output format' >/dev/null

python -c 'import importlib.metadata as md; assert md.version("weblogo") == "3.9.0"; import weblogo, numpy, scipy, importlib_resources, importlib_metadata; print("weblogo-runtime-ok")' | grep -F 'weblogo-runtime-ok' >/dev/null && gs --version >/dev/null && pdf2svg 2>&1 | grep -F 'Usage' >/dev/null

tmp=/tmp/taf-weblogo-logo-$$; rm -rf "$tmp"; mkdir -p "$tmp"; printf '%s\n' '>s1' 'ACGTACGA' '>s2' 'ACGTTCGA' '>s3' 'ACGTACCA' > "$tmp/seq.fa"; weblogo -f "$tmp/seq.fa" --sequence-type dna --title 'TAF WebLogo smoke' -F eps -o "$tmp/logo.eps"; grep -F '%%Creator:      WebLogo 3.9.0' "$tmp/logo.eps" >/dev/null; weblogo -f "$tmp/seq.fa" --sequence-type dna -F png -o "$tmp/logo.png"; file "$tmp/logo.png" | grep -F 'PNG image data' >/dev/null; weblogo -f "$tmp/seq.fa" --sequence-type dna -F pdf -o "$tmp/logo.pdf"; file "$tmp/logo.pdf" | grep -F 'PDF document' >/dev/null; weblogo -f "$tmp/seq.fa" --sequence-type dna -F svg -o "$tmp/logo.svg"; grep -F '<svg' "$tmp/logo.svg" >/dev/null; rm -rf "$tmp"

tmp=/tmp/taf-weblogo-transform-$$; rm -rf "$tmp"; mkdir -p "$tmp"; printf '%s\n' '>s1' 'ACGTACGA' '>s2' 'ACGTTCGA' > "$tmp/seq.fa"; transformseq -f "$tmp/seq.fa" --reverse -o "$tmp/reverse.fa"; grep -F 'AGCATGCA' "$tmp/reverse.fa" >/dev/null; weblogo --serve --port 18080 > "$tmp/server.log" 2>&1 & pid=$!; ok=0; for i in 1 2 3 4 5; do if curl -fsS http://127.0.0.1:18080/ > "$tmp/index.html" 2>/dev/null; then ok=1; break; fi; sleep 1; done; kill "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true; wait "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true; test "$ok" = 1; grep -F 'WebLogo' "$tmp/index.html" >/dev/null; rm -rf "$tmp"

upstream.name: WebLogo
upstream.citation: Crooks et al. 2004, WebLogo: A sequence logo generator
